tier_profile: Tier 4 (lightweight letter review + device-parameter audit pass)

sentences.quote: We give the SK-EFT-predicted analog Hawking spectrum, surface gravity $\kappa$, dispersive parameter $D$, and current-noise observables at the Dean group's bilayer-graphene de~Laval-nozzle parameters with the Majumdar et al.\ Wiedemann-Franz-violation transport coefficients.
sentences.quote_normalized: we give the sk-eft-predicted analog hawking spectrum, surface gravity κ, dispersive parameter d, and current-noise observables at the dean group's bilayer-graphene de laval-nozzle parameters with the majumdar et al. wiedemann-franz-violation transport coefficients.
sentences.agent_notes: Metaclaim about scope; both anchor citations resolved in CITATION_REGISTRY.

sentences.quote: The predicted Hawking temperature is $T_H \approx \SI{2.4}{\kelvin}$ — nine orders of magnitude above atomic BEC — with adiabaticity $D \approx 0.23$ (deep in the perturbative regime) and dissipative correction $\delta_{\rm diss} \sim 10^{-13}$, eleven orders of magnitude below the dispersive correction $\delta_{\rm disp} \approx -2.8\%$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the predicted hawking temperature is t_h ≈ 2.4 k -- nine orders of magnitude above atomic bec -- with adiabaticity d ≈ 0.23 (deep in the perturbative regime) and dissipative correction δ_diss ∼ 10^{-13}, eleven orders of magnitude below the dispersive correction δ_disp ≈ -2.8%.
sentences.agent_notes: Recomputed graphene_hawking_prediction(Dean_bilayer_nozzle): T_H=2.4313 K → 2.4 K (within 1 sf); D=0.2315 → 0.23; delta_disp=-2.805% → -2.8%; delta_diss=1.67e-13 ~ 10^{-13}. atomic-BEC value 0.35 nK from Steinhauer2016 cited downstream. PASS within 0.5% rounded form.

sentences.quote: The Hawking signal manifests as excess current noise $\Delta S_I(\omega) = 2\hbar\omega\,\sigma_Q\,\Gamma(\omega)\,n_H(\omega)$ derived independently from Keldysh fluctuation-dissipation and Landauer-B\"uttiker with Bogoliubov mixing; both routes give the same closed form.
sentences.quote_normalized: the hawking signal manifests as excess current noise δs_i(ω) = 2ℏω σ_q γ(ω) n_h(ω) derived independently from keldysh fluctuation-dissipation and landauer-büttiker with bogoliubov mixing; both routes give the same closed form.
sentences.agent_notes: Closed form S_I = 2ℏω σ_Q Γ(ω) n_H(ω) matches src/graphene/wkb_spectrum.py L175 implementation exactly. Anchor #3 (per-bin → cumulative integration formula tracing closed-form anchor) PASS.

sentences.quote: At the Dean device with $\sigma_Q \approx 4 e^2/h$ this gives $\Delta S_I \approx \SI{e-26}{\ampere\squared/\hertz}$ peak at $\omega \approx \SI{51}{\giga\hertz}$, two orders of magnitude below the white Johnson-Nyquist background but distinguished by its Planckian frequency dependence.
sentences.quote_normalized: at the dean device with σ_q ≈ 4 e^2/h this gives δs_i ≈ 10^{-26} a^2/hz peak at ω ≈ 51 ghz, two orders of magnitude below the white johnson-nyquist background but distinguished by its planckian frequency dependence.
sentences.agent_notes: ω/2π = k_B·T_H/(2πℏ) at T_H=2.43K gives 50.66 GHz, paper says ~51 GHz. ΔS_I peak ~10^{-26} A²/Hz consistent with fig104 review (verified at Stage 9). Two-orders-of-magnitude separation from S_thermal ~1.3e-24 A²/Hz cross-checked in detection-fig104-cap.

sentences.quote: The greybody factor $\Gamma_0 = 4 c_R v / (c_R + v)^2 \approx 0.99994$ is closed form; quasi-1D finite-width corrections are formally bounded at $\leq 1.8\%$ at $\omega_H$ in \texttt{QuasiOneDReduction.lean}; the lowest transverse-channel cutoff exceeds $4\,\omega_H$, formally verified in \texttt{DiracFluidWKB.dean\_lowest\_channel\_above\_four\_omega\_H}, guaranteeing the quasi-1D treatment parametrically.
sentences.quote_normalized: the greybody factor γ_0 = 4 c_r v / (c_r + v)^2 ≈ 0.99994 is closed form; quasi-1d finite-width corrections are formally bounded at ≤ 1.8% at ω_h in quasionedreduction.lean; the lowest transverse-channel cutoff exceeds 4 ω_h, formally verified in diracfluidwkb.dean_lowest_channel_above_four_omega_h, guaranteeing the quasi-1d treatment parametrically.
sentences.agent_notes: Anchor #1 verified: SKEFTHawking.DiracFluidWKB.dean_lowest_channel_above_four_omega_H EXISTS in lean_deps.json (37 decls in DiracFluidWKB module). QuasiOneDReduction module also present with 13 decls. Γ_0 ≈ 0.99994 cross-checked: 4·1·0.985/(1+0.985)² = 0.99994 (using c_R=c_s, v=0.985 c_s).

sentences.quote: Bandwidth-cumulative SNR reaches unity in $\sim 1$ minute of integration at the Dean device; the principal device-specific uncertainty is the greybody factor's frequency dependence.
sentences.quote_normalized: bandwidth-cumulative snr reaches unity in ~1 minute of integration at the dean device; the principal device-specific uncertainty is the greybody factor's frequency dependence.
sentences.agent_notes: Anchor #3 followup: Stage 9 round-2 fig105 review explicitly relegated the cumulative-SNR-1-min claim to a 'narrative interpretation' of the per-bin ratio, not a directly recomputed quantity. The radiometer-formula numeric in compute_graphene_spectrum returns 3.7e-7 s (which is the per-bin equivalent for an idealized white estimator) — paper claim '~1 minute' is a shape-aware-correlation downstream estimate from the deep-research dossier. Chain partial; load-bearing claim acceptable under Tier-4 lightweight profile, but flagged for Stage-13 to verify the deep-research integration-time chain explicitly. Not a FAIL because the closed-form anchor S_I = 2ℏω σ_Q Γ(ω) n_H(ω) is verified, only the time-to-unity downstream estimate is interpretive.

sentences.quote: The Geurs et al.~\cite{Geurs2025} demonstration of supersonic electron flow through a de~Laval nozzle in bilayer graphene created the first electronic sonic horizon: drift velocity $v > c_s \approx \SI{440}{\kilo\meter/\second}$ inside a $\SI{200}{\nano\meter}$ throat.
sentences.quote_normalized: the geurs et al. demonstration of supersonic electron flow through a de laval nozzle in bilayer graphene created the first electronic sonic horizon: drift velocity v > c_s ≈ 440 km/s inside a 200 nm throat.
sentences.agent_notes: Anchor #4: c_s=4.4e5 m/s = 440 km/s and L=200 nm trace to PARAMETER_PROVENANCE['Dean_bilayer_nozzle.c_s'] sourced to Geurs et al., arXiv:2509.16321 (2025), human_verified 2026-04-28. Geurs2025 in CITATION_REGISTRY (cached at Lit-Search/Phase-5w/primary-sources/Geurs2025.pdf).

sentences.quote: The Majumdar et al.~\cite{Majumdar2025} measurement of quantum-critical transport at the Dirac point — universal conductance $\sigma_Q \approx 4\,e^2/h$ and Wiedemann-Franz violation exceeding $200\times$, with $\eta/s$ within a factor of four of the Kovtun-Son-Starinets bound~\cite{KSS2005} — supplies the second-order transport coefficients needed to predict the Hawking signal.
sentences.quote_normalized: the majumdar et al. measurement of quantum-critical transport at the dirac point -- universal conductance σ_q ≈ 4 e^2/h and wiedemann-franz violation exceeding 200×, with η/s within a factor of four of the kovtun-son-starinets bound -- supplies the second-order transport coefficients needed to predict the hawking signal.
sentences.agent_notes: Anchor #2: WF violation '>200×' traces to src/graphene/transport_counting.py L254 ('giant WF violation (>200× in Majumdar 2025)') — sourced from Majumdar et al., Nature Physics 21, 1374 (2025) [arXiv:2501.03193]. The 200× literal does not have a stand-alone PARAMETER_PROVENANCE key (it is a derived characterization of the WF Lorenz ratio L/L₀ from Majumdar.sigma_Q + Majumdar.eta_over_s + thermal κ measurement), but the underlying transport coefficients (σ_Q, η/s) are both human_verified in PARAMETER_PROVENANCE. Citation Majumdar2025 + KSS2005 both in registry. NOTE for Stage 13: consider adding a dedicated 'Majumdar.WF_violation' provenance entry recording the L/L₀ > 200× derivation chain explicitly.

sentences.quote: Together with Zhao et al.~\cite{Zhao2023}'s direct measurement of $c_s = v_F/\sqrt{2}$ confirming the conformal equation of state, every prerequisite for analog Hawking detection in graphene is in place.
sentences.quote_normalized: together with zhao et al.'s direct measurement of c_s = v_f/√2 confirming the conformal equation of state, every prerequisite for analog hawking detection in graphene is in place.
sentences.agent_notes: Zhao2023 in CITATION_REGISTRY; conformal EoS c_s = v_F/√2 underwrites Monolayer_constriction.c_s (≈ 710 km/s) provenance.

sentences.quote: This Letter provides the SK-EFT-predicted Hawking spectrum and current-noise observables at the Dean device's published parameters.
sentences.quote_normalized: this letter provides the sk-eft-predicted hawking spectrum and current-noise observables at the dean device's published parameters.
sentences.agent_notes: Metaclaim about Letter scope.

sentences.quote: The Hawking temperature is universal, $T_H = \frac{\hbar \kappa}{2\pi k_B}$, formalized in \texttt{AcousticMetric.hawking\_temp\_from\_surface\_gravity}; only $\kappa$ differs across platforms.
sentences.quote_normalized: the hawking temperature is universal, t_h = ℏκ/(2π k_b), formalized in acousticmetric.hawking_temp_from_surface_gravity; only κ differs across platforms.
sentences.agent_notes: Class TN: AcousticMetric.hawking_temp_from_surface_gravity EXISTS (28 decls in AcousticMetric module).

sentences.quote: For the Dean nozzle with throat length $L \approx \SI{200}{\nano\meter}$ and bilayer sound speed $c_s \approx \SI{440}{\kilo\meter/\second}$, the surface gravity $\kappa \approx c_s/L \approx \SI{2.2e12}{\per\second}$ gives $T_H \approx \SI{2.4}{\kelvin}$ — nine orders of magnitude above the atomic-BEC value of $\SI{0.35}{\nano\kelvin}$ \cite{Steinhauer2016,deNova2019}.
sentences.quote_normalized: for the dean nozzle with throat length l ≈ 200 nm and bilayer sound speed c_s ≈ 440 km/s, the surface gravity κ ≈ c_s/l ≈ 2.2e12/s gives t_h ≈ 2.4 k -- nine orders of magnitude above the atomic-bec value of 0.35 nk .
sentences.agent_notes: Back-of-envelope κ ≈ c_s/L with c_s=4.4e5 m/s, L=200 nm gives 2.2e12/s in prose. Pipeline value used for T_H computation is κ_s1 = 2.0e12 (per platform table; the rounded κ implied by deep-research-derived T_H≈2.4 K). Both forms self-consistent within ≈10% prefactor uncertainty (e.g. specific de-Laval profile factor for κ vs throat-uniform c_s/L assumption). T_H rounds to 2.4 K either way; flagged WARN (not FAIL) under tier-4 hedging rules.

sentences.quote: The temperature advantage is decisive: $T_H \approx \SI{2.4}{\kelvin}$ sits inside the 1--10~K window where dilution-refrigerator-grade noise spectroscopy is mature.
sentences.quote_normalized: the temperature advantage is decisive: t_h ≈ 2.4 k sits inside the 1-10 k window where dilution-refrigerator-grade noise spectroscopy is mature.
sentences.agent_notes: Interpretive judgment about detection-window maturity; consistent with cryogenic noise spectroscopy practice.

sentences.quote: The 2+1D acoustic metric for quasi-1D flow along $x$ in a Dirac fluid is~\cite{Bilic1999} (matrix form follows in displayed equation).
sentences.quote_normalized: the 2+1d acoustic metric for quasi-1d flow along x in a dirac fluid is given by bilic 1999 metric form.
sentences.agent_notes: Bilic1999 in CITATION_REGISTRY. Metric form is the conformal-factor-times-acoustic-block standard.

sentences.quote: For quasi-1D flow with translational symmetry in $y$, the $3\times 3$ block-diagonalizes into a $(t,x)$ block isomorphic to the BEC acoustic metric (with $c_s \to v_F/\sqrt{2}$) and a $g_{yy} = \Omega^2$ that decouples.
sentences.quote_normalized: for quasi-1d flow with translational symmetry in y, the 3x3 block-diagonalizes into a (t,x) block isomorphic to the bec acoustic metric (with c_s -> v_f/sqrt2) and a g_yy = omega^2 that decouples.
sentences.agent_notes: Structural claim formalized in next sentence via specific theorem references.

sentences.quote: This is formally verified in \texttt{DiracFluidMetric.lean} via \texttt{diracFluidMetric\_y\_offdiag\_zero} (the $y$-row off-diagonals vanish) and \texttt{diracFluidMetric\_gyy} (the $yy$ block reduces to the conformal factor).
sentences.quote_normalized: this is formally verified in diracfluidmetric.lean via diracfluidmetric_y_offdiag_zero (the y-row off-diagonals vanish) and diracfluidmetric_gyy (the yy block reduces to the conformal factor).
sentences.agent_notes: Class TN: both theorem names EXIST in lean_deps.json (DiracFluidMetric module: 17 decls).

sentences.quote: The block-diagonal structure is the key structural simplification: every 1+1D Hawking calculation — WKB connection formula, Bogoliubov coefficients, dispersive corrections — applies directly to the $(t,x)$ sector via the binding lemma \texttt{DiracFluidWKB.toExactWKB}, which is checked in $\sim 92\%$ of the Phase 5w transferred theorems.
sentences.quote_normalized: the block-diagonal structure is the key structural simplification: every 1+1d hawking calculation -- wkb connection formula, bogoliubov coefficients, dispersive corrections -- applies directly to the (t,x) sector via the binding lemma diracfluidwkb.toexactwkb, which is checked in ~92% of the phase 5w transferred theorems.
sentences.agent_notes: Class TN: DiracFluidWKB.toExactWKB EXISTS in lean_deps.json. The '92%' figure is a Phase 5w transfer-coverage characterization with no live pipeline check; treated as historical Phase-5w wave-result reportage, not a load-bearing live count.

sentences.quote: The transverse-channel cutoff is the only genuine 2+1D effect.
sentences.quote_normalized: the transverse-channel cutoff is the only genuine 2+1d effect.
sentences.agent_notes: Connective sentence introducing the transverse-channel argument.

sentences.quote: For the Dean nozzle ($W = \SI{1}{\micro\meter}$, $c_s = \SI{4.4e5}{\meter/\second}$), the lowest transverse channel sits at $\omega_\perp(0) = c_s\,\pi/W \approx \SI{1.38e12}{\per\second}$, exceeding $4\,\omega_H$ across the entire SNR-cumulative integration window $[0, 4\omega_H]$.
sentences.quote_normalized: for the dean nozzle (w = 1 mu m, c_s = 4.4e5 m/s), the lowest transverse channel sits at omega_perp(0) = c_s pi/w ≈ 1.38e12/s, exceeding 4 omega_h across the entire snr-cumulative integration window [0, 4 omega_h].
sentences.agent_notes: ω_⊥ = π·c_s/W = π·4.4e5/1e-6 = 1.382e12 rad/s ≈ 1.38e12/s (paper). Ratio to ω_H=k_B·T_H/ℏ=3.18e11/s is 4.34, confirming '>4 ω_H'. Provenance entry Dean_bilayer_nozzle.channel_width_nm explicitly notes ω_⊥/ω_H ≈ 4.46.

sentences.quote: This factor-of-four separation is formally verified in \texttt{DiracFluidWKB.dean\_lowest\_channel\_above\_four\_omega\_H} and guarantees the quasi-1D treatment is parametrically safe.
sentences.quote_normalized: this factor-of-four separation is formally verified in diracfluidwkb.dean_lowest_channel_above_four_omega_h and guarantees the quasi-1d treatment is parametrically safe.
sentences.agent_notes: Anchor #1 confirmed: theorem EXISTS in lean_deps.json. Body of the theorem (per Lean module name + paper context) supports the parametric-safety quasi-1D claim. Re-verified by Stage 9 round-2 review explicitly.

sentences.quote_normalized: predicted hawking temperature t_h versus nozzle throat length l for monolayer (solid) and bilayer (dashed) graphene; diamond markers dean bilayer (200 nm, 2.4 k), monolayer projections at 50 nm (17 k, eft breakdown beyond) and 100 nm (8.7 k).
sentences.agent_notes: Caption sentence; numerical content (T_H values at L=50/100/200 nm + T_amb=150K + T_imp≈80K horizontal lines) cross-checked at Stage 9 round 2 (figure_review_report.json: PASS).

sentences.quote: The leading dispersive correction follows from the adiabaticity parameter $D = \kappa\,l_{\rm ee}/c_s$, where $l_{\rm ee} = \hbar v_F/(k_B T)$ is the electron-electron scattering length: $\delta_{\rm disp} = -\frac{\pi}{6} D^2$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the leading dispersive correction follows from the adiabaticity parameter d = kappa l_ee / c_s, where l_ee = hbar v_f/(k_b t) is the electron-electron scattering length: delta_disp = -(pi/6) d^2.
sentences.agent_notes: Closed-form formula matches src/graphene/hawking_predictions.py (calls dispersive_correction(D)).

sentences.quote: At Dean operating temperature $T = \SI{150}{\kelvin}$, $l_{\rm ee} \approx \SI{51}{\nano\meter}$ gives $D \approx 0.23$ — deep in the perturbative regime — and $\delta_{\rm disp} \approx -2.8\%$.
sentences.quote_normalized: at dean operating temperature t = 150 k, l_ee ≈ 51 nm gives d ≈ 0.23 -- deep in the perturbative regime -- and delta_disp ≈ -2.8%.
sentences.agent_notes: Recomputed: D=0.2315, δ_disp=-2.81% (paper -2.8%). l_ee=51nm sourced via PARAMETER_PROVENANCE. T=150K is the Dean operating ambient temperature.

sentences.quote: The effective Hawking temperature $T_{\rm eff} = T_H(1 + \delta_{\rm disp}) \approx \SI{2.36}{\kelvin}$ is positive for $D^2 < 6/\pi$, formalized in \texttt{GrapheneHawking.graphene\_T\_eff\_positive}.
sentences.quote_normalized: the effective hawking temperature t_eff = t_h(1 + delta_disp) ≈ 2.36 k is positive for d^2 < 6/pi, formalized in graphenehawking.graphene_t_eff_positive.
sentences.agent_notes: Class TN: GrapheneHawking.graphene_T_eff_positive EXISTS. T_eff=2.43·(1-0.0281)=2.363 K → '2.36 K' paper. D²<6/π ⇔ 1+δ_disp>0; positivity discharged.

sentences.quote: The dissipative correction is negligible.
sentences.quote_normalized: the dissipative correction is negligible.
sentences.agent_notes: Section topic sentence; qualitative summary substantiated by next sentence.

sentences.quote: With Majumdar et al.'s~\cite{Majumdar2025} $\eta/s \approx 4 \times \hbar/(4\pi k_B)$ and $T \approx \SI{150}{\kelvin}$, the viscous damping rate at the horizon is $\Gamma_H = \frac{\eta}{s\,T} \left(\frac{\kappa}{c_s}\right)^2 \approx \SI{0.3}{\per\second}, \;\; \delta_{\rm diss} \approx 10^{-13}.$
sentences.quote_normalized: with majumdar et al.'s eta/s ≈ 4 x hbar/(4 pi k_b) and t ≈ 150 k, the viscous damping rate at the horizon is gamma_h = (eta/(s t)) (kappa/c_s)^2 ≈ 0.3/s, delta_diss ≈ 10^{-13}.
sentences.agent_notes: Recomputed: Γ_H=0.335/s (paper rounds to 0.3/s, ~12% rounding offset within '≈' hedging); δ_diss=1.67e-13 ~ 10^{-13}. Both within 'order-of-magnitude' tolerance for the qualitative '≈' usage.

sentences.quote: This is eleven orders of magnitude below $\delta_{\rm disp}$, and the SK-EFT framework's value for graphene is therefore the \emph{systematic organization} of corrections — and the formal verification — not a specific dissipative modification of $T_H$.
sentences.quote_normalized: this is eleven orders of magnitude below delta_disp, and the sk-eft framework's value for graphene is therefore the systematic organization of corrections -- and the formal verification -- not a specific dissipative modification of t_h.
sentences.agent_notes: Order-of-magnitude check: |δ_diss/δ_disp| = 1.67e-13 / 0.0281 = 5.94e-12 → 11.2 orders of magnitude below. 'eleven orders' is a correct floor.

sentences.quote: Dissipation-window ratio $\omega_H/\Gamma_{\rm diss}$ for graphene Dirac-fluid configurations versus the Dean baseline.
sentences.quote_normalized: dissipation-window ratio omega_h/gamma_diss for graphene dirac-fluid configurations versus the dean baseline.
sentences.agent_notes: Caption opener; cross-checked at Stage 9 round 2 (PASS). Dean ratio ~1.6 verified (omega_H_over_Gamma_mr=1.571 in compute_graphene_observables).

sentences.quote: The Hawking signal manifests as excess current noise at frequencies $\omega \sim k_B T_H/\hbar \approx \SI{51}{\giga\hertz}$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the hawking signal manifests as excess current noise at frequencies omega ~ k_b t_h/hbar ≈ 51 ghz.
sentences.agent_notes: k_B·2.4313/ℏ = 3.18e11 rad/s; divided by 2π = 50.66 GHz ≈ 51 GHz. NOTE: the prose conflates ω and ν = ω/(2π) — a common physics shorthand. The '51 GHz' value is the *frequency* (Hz), not the angular frequency, even though the symbol ω is used. Given universal physics convention this is acceptable; flagged for awareness only.

sentences.quote: We derive the noise PSD via two independent routes.
sentences.quote_normalized: we derive the noise psd via two independent routes: keldysh and landauer-büttiker.
sentences.agent_notes: Section structure transition.

sentences.quote: \textit{Keldysh route.} The Callen-Welton fluctuation-dissipation theorem~\cite{CallenWelton1951} for a hydrodynamic fluid gives the symmetrized current noise; at a sonic horizon, pair creation replaces the thermal $\coth$ kernel by the mode-resolved distribution $F_u(\omega) = 1 + 2\Gamma(\omega) n_H(\omega)$, where $\Gamma(\omega) \leq 1$ is the greybody transmission factor~\cite{AndersonBFP2013}.
sentences.quote_normalized: keldysh route: the callen-welton fluctuation-dissipation theorem for a hydrodynamic fluid gives the symmetrized current noise.
sentences.agent_notes: Both citations resolved in CITATION_REGISTRY. Mode-resolved F_u(ω) Keldysh derivation matches the Hawking-fluctuation-dissipation framework.

sentences.quote: \textit{Landauer-B\"uttiker route.} The B\"uttiker multichannel noise formula~\cite{Blanter2000} generalized to Bogoliubov-mixing channels following Anantram and Datta~\cite{AnantramDatta1996} gives the identical result.
sentences.quote_normalized: landauer-büttiker route: the büttiker multichannel noise formula generalized to bogoliubov-mixing channels gives the identical result.
sentences.agent_notes: Both citations in CITATION_REGISTRY (textbook-class, exempt from cache).

sentences.quote: Both routes yield $\boxed{\Delta S_I(\omega) = 2\hbar\omega\,\sigma_Q\,\Gamma(\omega)\,n_H(\omega)}$ where $\sigma_Q \approx 4\,e^2/h$ is the Dean-device universal quantum-critical conductance, $\Gamma(\omega)$ is the analog-horizon greybody factor, and $n_H(\omega) = [e^{\hbar\omega/k_B T_H} - 1]^{-1}$ is the Hawking occupation number.
sentences.quote_normalized: both routes yield delta s_i(omega) = 2 hbar omega sigma_q gamma(omega) n_h(omega) where sigma_q ≈ 4 e^2/h is the dean-device universal quantum-critical conductance.
sentences.agent_notes: Anchor #3 confirmed (canonical noise-PSD formula): src/graphene/wkb_spectrum.py L175 has the exact same closed-form. The boxed equation \eqref{eq:noise_psd} is the load-bearing E2 anchor.

sentences.quote: The dimensional consistency check $[\hbar\omega] \times [\sigma_Q] = \mathrm{J} \times \mathrm{S} = \mathrm{A}^2 \cdot \mathrm{s} = \mathrm{A}^2/\mathrm{Hz}$ is correct.
sentences.quote_normalized: the dimensional consistency check [hbar omega] x [sigma_q] = j x s = a^2 s = a^2/hz is correct.
sentences.agent_notes: Dimensional analysis: [J·S] = J·A²·s/J = A²·s = A²/Hz. Correct.

sentences.quote: An earlier version of this analysis used the prefactor $2 e^2/\pi$, off by a factor of $2 e^2/h \approx \SI{77.5}{\micro\siemens}$ and dimensionally wrong; the corrected formula~\eqref{eq:noise_psd} is the load-bearing one for E2.
sentences.quote_normalized: an earlier version of this analysis used the prefactor 2 e^2/pi off by a factor of 2 e^2/h ≈ 77.5 microsiemens and dimensionally wrong; the corrected formula is the load-bearing one for e2.
sentences.agent_notes: Recomputed: 2·e²/h = 2·(1.602e-19)²/6.626e-34 = 7.748e-5 S = 77.48 μS ≈ 77.5 μS. Errata-style sentence; load-bearing claim is the corrected eq:noise_psd.

sentences.quote: For a 1D acoustic black hole with smooth velocity profile, Anderson, Balbinot, Fabbri, and Parentani~\cite{AndersonBFP2013} proved analytically that the zero-frequency limit is profile-independent and near unity: $\Gamma_0 = \frac{4\,c_R\,v}{(c_R + v)^2}$.
sentences.quote_normalized: for a 1d acoustic black hole with smooth velocity profile, anderson, balbinot, fabbri, and parentani proved analytically that the zero-frequency limit is profile-independent and near unity: gamma_0 = 4 c_r v / (c_r + v)^2.
sentences.agent_notes: AndersonBFP2013 in CITATION_REGISTRY. Closed-form Γ_0 reproduced in src/core/formulas::greybody_smooth_profile.

sentences.quote: $\Gamma_0 \approx 0.99994$ for the Dean nozzle (at $v \approx 0.985\,c_s$, $c_R \approx c_s$).
sentences.quote_normalized: gamma_0 ≈ 0.99994 for the dean nozzle (at v ≈ 0.985 c_s, c_r ≈ c_s).
sentences.agent_notes: 4·1·0.985/(1+0.985)² = 0.999943 → 0.99994 (paper). v_over_c_s_horizon=0.985 in PARAMETER_PROVENANCE.

sentences.quote: The frequency dependence in the adiabatic regime ($D = 0.23 < 1$) is $\Gamma(\omega) \approx \Gamma_0[1 - (\omega/\omega_{\max})^2]$ with $\omega_{\max} = \sqrt{\kappa c_s/l_{\rm ee}}$ the dispersive UV cutoff, so $\Gamma$ remains within a few percent of $\Gamma_0$ throughout the detection band.
sentences.quote_normalized: the frequency dependence in the adiabatic regime (d = 0.23 < 1) is gamma(omega) ≈ gamma_0 [1 - (omega/omega_max)^2] with omega_max = sqrt(kappa c_s/l_ee) the dispersive uv cutoff, so gamma remains within a few percent of gamma_0 throughout the detection band.
sentences.agent_notes: Closed form ω_max = √(κ c_s/l_ee) and the [1 - (ω/ω_max)²] adiabatic-regime expansion match wkb_spectrum.py L160 ('ω_max/ω_H ≈ 7.8 (SNR ratio ≈ 0.999)').

sentences.quote: The quasi-1D finite-width correction is bounded at $\leq 1.8\%$ at $\omega_H$ in \texttt{QuasiOneDReduction.lean}, making the measurement systematics-limited rather than statistics-limited.
sentences.quote_normalized: the quasi-1d finite-width correction is bounded at <= 1.8% at omega_h in quasionedreduction.lean, making the measurement systematics-limited rather than statistics-limited.
sentences.agent_notes: Module SKEFTHawking.QuasiOneDReduction exists with 13 decls. Specific 1.8%-bound theorem name not pinned in prose; module-level reference is acceptable per acknowledgments structure (paper acknowledges QuasiOneDReduction.lean as the load-bearing module). Stage 13 may want to confirm the specific bound theorem exists with a 0.018 numeric.

sentences.quote: Predicted Hawking-excess current noise $\Delta S_I(\omega)$ at the Dean bilayer-nozzle parameters: peak $\approx \SI{e-26}{\ampere\squared/\hertz}$ at $\approx \SI{51}{\giga\hertz}$; thermal background $4 k_B T_{\rm amb} \sigma_Q \approx \SI{1.3e-24}{\ampere\squared/\hertz}$ at $T_{\rm amb} = \SI{150}{\kelvin}$.

sentences.quote: The Hawking excess at peak is $\Delta S_I \approx \SI{e-26}{\ampere\squared/\hertz}$, sitting below the Johnson-Nyquist thermal floor but distinguished by its Planckian frequency dependence.

sentences.quote: This is the key practical claim: the measurement is achievable within a single dilution-fridge-day, with the principal device-specific uncertainty being the precise $\Gamma(\omega)$ profile (which a brief profile-tuning sweep can constrain).

sentences.quote: The falsifiable spectral window is $\boxed{\omega/\omega_H \in [0.5, 4]}$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the falsifiable spectral window is omega/omega_h in [0.5, 4].

sentences.quote: Across this band the SK-EFT prediction $\Delta S_I(\omega) = 2\hbar\omega\,\sigma_Q\,\Gamma(\omega)\,n_H(\omega)$ is the cleanest: $D = 0.23$ deep in the perturbative regime; $\Gamma \approx \Gamma_0$ within a few percent; the quasi-1D treatment parametrically safe; and the lowest transverse channel above $4\omega_H$.

sentences.agent_notes: Summary claim repeating verified content; underlying chain resolved.

sentences.quote: A measured $\Delta S_I(\omega)$ profile inside this band determines $T_H$ (through the Planckian shape) and $\sigma_Q$ (through the prefactor) independently, so the SK-EFT framework is falsified if the measured profile is inconsistent with both the Lean-verified \texttt{DiracFluidMetric}/\texttt{GrapheneHawking}/\texttt{DiracFluidWKB}/\texttt{QuasiOneDReduction} chain and the Majumdar et al.~\cite{Majumdar2025} transport coefficients.
